data.title: PLATTS: 3051--Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) Revenue Indices - 14Nov25
COSCIENS Biopharma Names Puccetti Interim CEO After Biehn Resigns
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

COSCIENS Biopharma named Peter Puccetti interim chief executive officer after Anna Biehn stepped down from the role, effective immediately.

Puccetti, who will remain chairman of the Canadian pharmaceutical company's board, will oversee the continuing strategic reorientation of the COSCIENS, board member Robert Seager said Friday.

"As the company continues its efforts to reduce costs and streamline its organizational structure while aligning resources with key strategic priorities, the board has determined that now is the right time to transition leadership," Seager said.

Biehn's resignation comes after the company on Tuesday posted a third-quarter loss of $1.8 million, or 57 cents a share, compared with a loss of $5.8 million, or $1.85 per share, a year earlier.

Revenue was $1.5 million, down from $1.9 million a year prior.

The company said its strategic restructuring adopted in the second quarter, which involved moving to a zero-based budgeting initiative and reducing employee headcount, lead to a 59% year-over-year decrease in operating expenses for the quarter.

Shares fell to a 52-week low of $2.85 on Wednesday and the stock is down almost 15% this year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Blue Water Acquisition Corp. III to Suspend Participation in Citgo Acquisition Process
Talon Metals Reports Results for the Quarter Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Road Town, Tortola, British Virgin Islands--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) - Talon Metals Corp.  ("Talon" or the "Company") reported net income for the three months ended September 30, 2025 of $1,676 or nil per share (basic and diluted), which was primarily the result of administration expenses offset by foreign currency gains and interest income. This compares to a net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2024 of $0.6 million or nil per share (basic and diluted), which was primarily the result of administration expenses and stock option compensation.

The Company's net loss for the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 was $2.0 million or nil per share (basic and diluted). This compares to a net loss of $1.6 million or nil per share (basic and diluted) for the same period in the prior year.

Capitalized exploration and development costs and deferred expenditures on the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project for the three months ended September 30, 2025 amounted to $5.6 million, primarily the result of exploration and development costs and deferred expenditures of $7.4 million offset by governments grants received of $1.8 million. This compares to capitalized exploration and development costs and deferred expenditures on the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project for the three months ended September 30, 2024 which amounted to a credit of $3.7 million, primarily the result of a sale of a royalty for net proceeds of $10.5 million and governments grants received of $0.7 million offset by capitalized exploration and development costs and deferred expenditures of $7.6 million. The total capitalized cost to the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project to September 30, 2025 amounts to $240.8 million.

Condensed Interim Consolidated Financial Statements for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025 and 2024, together with Management's Discussion and Analysis have been filed on SEDAR+ and are available at www.sedarplus.ca.

All amounts are presented in Canadian dollars.

ABOUT TALON

Talon is a TSX-listed base metals company in a joint venture with Rio Tinto on the high-grade Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project located in central Minnesota. Talon's shares are also traded in the US over the OTC market under the symbol TLOFF. The Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project comprises a large land position (18km of strike length) with additional high-grade intercepts outside the current resource area. Talon has an earn-in right to acquire up to 60% of the Tamarack Nickel Project and currently owns 51%. Talon has a neutrality and workforce development agreement in place with the United Steelworkers union. Talon's Battery Mineral Processing Facility in Mercer County was selected by the US Department of Energy for US$114.8 million funding grant from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law and the US Department of Defense awarded Talon a grant of US$20.6 million to support and accelerate Talon's exploration efforts in both Minnesota and Michigan. Talon has well-qualified experienced exploration, mine development, external affairs and mine permitting teams.

For additional information on Talon, please visit the Company's website at www.talonmetals.com or contact:

Mike Kicis

President

Talon Metals Corp.

Tel: 1 (647) 968-0060

Email: kicis@talonmetals.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/274662

data.title: Investors SueWallSt as MoonLake Immunotherapeutics Faces Securities Fraud Allegations
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 14, 2025 / - SueWallSt: Class Action Filed Against MoonLake Immunotherapeutics - Contact Levi & Korsinsky

A Class Action lawsuit was filed on behalf of investors who lost money as a result of alleged securities fraud between March 10, 2024 and September 29, 2025.

If you suffered a loss in MoonLake Immunotherapeutics, don't stay silent. Take action now by visiting:

https://zlk.com/suewallst2?wire=1

If you have any questions about this class action lawsuit or any other sudden or unexpected stock loss, email lostmoney@suewallst.com or call Levi & Korsinsky at (888)-SUE-WALLST (783-9255).

Get more information on this and other class action lawsuits on the SueWallSt podcast and subscribe to the SueWallst WhatsApp channel for breaking class action news.

SueWallSt Channel

LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, the team at Levi & Korsinsky has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. Our firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States.

data.title: Shareholder Bailey Vicky Rev Tr Files To Sell 1,800 Of TXNM Energy Inc [TXNM]
data.description:
Bailey Vicky Rev Tr, Shareholder of TXNM Energy Inc [TXNM], filed a Form 144 on November 14, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission proposing to sell restricted securities:

Approximate Date Of Sale11/14/25
Shares1,800
BrokerMorgan Stanley Smith Barney LLC

Prior to the sale of restricted stock, owners must file a Form 144 with the SEC. Upon submission of the filing to the SEC the owner is permitted to sell the shares, or any fraction thereof, within 90 days.

data.title: Repare Therapeutics Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Repare Therapeutics Inc., a leading clinical-stage precision oncology company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments, underscoring significant strides in revenue generation and strategic realignments.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $11.6 million, driven by the Debiopharm License Agreement.

Income (loss) from operations: $1.1 million, reflecting gains from the termination of the Debiopharm collaboration agreement.

Net income (loss): $3.3 million, compared to a loss of $34.4 million in the prior year period, primarily due to revenue from the Debiopharm License Agreement and cost reductions.

Basic Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.08, compared to a loss of $0.81 per share in the prior year period.

Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $0.08, reflecting the positive net income achieved in the current period.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company recognized revenue from the Debiopharm License Agreement, which contributed significantly to the revenue for the period. This agreement involved an exclusive worldwide license for the product candidate lunresertib.

Geographical Performance: Revenue was primarily derived from Switzerland due to the Debiopharm License Agreement, highlighting the importance of this region in the company's operations.

New Product Launches: The company entered into an exclusive worldwide license agreement with Debiopharm for lunresertib, a first-in-class oncology PKMYT1 inhibitor, marking a significant development in their product offerings.

Future Outlook: The company expects to maintain a minimal workforce to finalize the transaction with XenoTherapeutics, Inc., which is anticipated to close in the first quarter of 2026. This strategic move is expected to impact the company's operational focus and resource allocation.

Strategic Re-Prioritization: The company announced a re-alignment of resources and a re-prioritization of its clinical portfolio to focus on the advancement of its Phase 1 clinical programs, RP-3467 and RP-1664, reflecting a shift in strategic focus.

Operational Adjustments: The company executed a phased reorganization plan to reduce its workforce by approximately 75% by the fourth quarter of 2025, aligning its resources with its strategic priorities.

Collaboration Termination: The Debiopharm Collaboration Agreement was terminated as part of the new license agreement, resulting in a gain on termination and reflecting a strategic shift in collaboration efforts.

SEC Filing: Repare Therapeutics Inc. [ RPTX ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: NEXTNRG, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
NEXTNRG, INC., a leading company in the sustainable energy and mobile fueling industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights substantial financial growth and strategic business initiatives aimed at expanding the company's market reach and operational capabilities.

Financial Highlights

Sales - net: NEXTNRG, INC. reported net sales of $58.82 million, a significant increase from $20.98 million in the same period last year. This growth was driven by expanded operations and increased customer demand.

Gross margin (loss): The company achieved a gross margin of $4.53 million, up from $1.62 million, indicating improved cost management and operational efficiency.

Loss from operations: The loss from operations was $(45.52) million, compared to $(7.45) million in the prior year period. This increase was primarily due to higher general and administrative expenses, including stock-based compensation.

Net loss: The net loss for the period was $(60.05) million, a substantial increase from $(18.84) million, impacted by higher interest expenses and loss on settlement of liabilities.

Basic and diluted loss per share: The basic and diluted loss per share was $(0.51), compared to $(0.18) in the previous year, reflecting the increased net loss and a higher weighted average number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Fuel sales accounted for 96.49% of total revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, indicating a strong focus on this product line.

Customer Concentration: A significant portion of sales, 48.85%, was attributed to a single customer, highlighting a potential risk in customer concentration.

Geographical Performance: The company continues to expand its national footprint, integrating sustainable energy solutions into its mobile fueling operations, which suggests a strategic focus on broadening market reach.

Sales Units: NEXTNRG, INC. sold 34 trucks in May 2025 and 35 trucks between August 4 and August 29, 2025, as part of its operational activities. These trucks were then leased back to support ongoing operations.

New Product Launches: The company is beginning to provide services as a renewable energy company, focusing on developing and deploying wireless electric vehicle charging technology integrated with battery storage and solar energy solutions.

New Production Launches: NEXTNRG, INC. executed an asset purchase agreement with Shell to acquire 73 vehicles and above-ground storage tanks, which were placed into service in January 2025, supporting its growth and expansion plan.

Future Outlook: The company plans to expand into new and existing markets, obtain additional financing for growth, and collaborate with other businesses for strategic opportunities, indicating a proactive approach to future growth and sustainability.

SEC Filing: NEXTNRG, INC. [ NXXT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: GENERATION INCOME PROPERTIES, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Generation Income Properties, Inc., a real estate investment trust (REIT) specializing in acquiring and managing income-generating properties, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities for the period ending September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Generation Income Properties, Inc. reported the following key financial metrics for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2025:

  • Total Revenue: $2.47 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting a slight increase from $2.40 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Total Revenue: $7.28 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $7.09 million in the same period in 2024.
  • Operating Loss: $(1.70) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.37) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Operating Loss: $(5.55) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(4.04) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(1.72) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(2.10) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(6.98) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(5.44) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(2.83) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(2.97) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(9.98) million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(8.15) million in the same period in 2024.
  • Basic & Diluted Loss Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(0.52) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.55) in the same period in 2024.
  • Basic & Diluted Loss Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(1.83) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.60) in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

The company's operational performance and key business activities for the third quarter of 2025 include:

  • Revenue Segments: Generation Income Properties derives its rental income from long-term net leases, with significant contributions from tenants such as the General Services Administration, Dollar General, Pre-K, Kohl's, and exp U.S. Services, each accounting for more than 10% of rental revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The company owns properties across various locations in the United States, including Norfolk, VA; Manteo, NC; Vacaville, CA; Tucson, AZ; and Maitland, FL, with notable tenant presence in these regions.
  • New Product Launches: On February 6, 2025, the company acquired three single-tenant retail properties in Sanford, FL, Cleveland, TN, and Kernersville, NC, leased to Zaxby's, Dollar General, and Tractor Supply Co., respectively, expanding its portfolio.
  • Real Estate Held for Sale: During the third quarter of 2025, the company approved a plan to sell three assets located in Grand Junction, CO; Plant City, FL; and Maitland, FL, with a combined cost basis of approximately $10.73 million.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on optimizing shareholder value and benefits through strategic initiatives, including refinancing, extending terms for preferred equity and loans, and divesting underperforming properties. A Special Committee has been appointed to evaluate opportunities to enhance shareholder value.

SEC Filing: GENERATION INCOME PROPERTIES, INC. [ GIPR ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Cineverse Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Cineverse Corp., a leading entertainment company specializing in digital streaming and theatrical releases, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the three and six months ended September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: Cineverse Corp. reported total revenue of $12.4 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, marking a 3% decrease compared to the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily due to lower licensing revenue. However, for the six months ended September 30, 2025, total revenue increased by 7% to $23.5 million, driven by growth in digital releases and subscription services.

Operating Loss: The company experienced an operating loss of $(5.4) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.9) million in the same period in 2024. This increase in operating loss was attributed to higher marketing and compensation expenses. For the six months ended September 30, 2025, the operating loss widened to $(9.1) million from $(3.6) million in the same period in 2024, due to similar factors.

Net Loss: Cineverse Corp. reported a net loss of $(5.5) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.2) million in the same period in 2024. The net loss for the six months ended September 30, 2025, was $(9.1) million, up from $(4.3) million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased operating expenses.

Net Loss Per Share: The net loss per share was $(0.31) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.09) in the same period in 2024. For the six months ended September 30, 2025, the net loss per share was $(0.53), compared to $(0.29) in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Streaming and Digital Revenue: The streaming and digital segment saw a decrease in revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to lower licensing revenue. However, the six-month period experienced an increase in revenue driven by the digital release of 'Terrifier 3' and growth in subscription-based platforms.

Base Distribution Revenue: There was a notable increase in base distribution revenue for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025. This growth was attributed to the theatrical release of 'Toxic Avenger' and the final theatrical revenue for 'Terrifier 3'.

Podcast and Other Revenue: This segment experienced a decline in revenue for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to lower podcast direct advertising. However, there was an increase in Matchpoint™ revenue.

Direct Operating Expenses: Direct operating expenses decreased for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025. This reduction was driven by lower variable costs such as royalty expenses and platform fees, despite increased theatrical distribution fees related to 'Toxic Avenger'.

Selling, General and Administrative Expenses: These expenses increased significantly for both the three and six months ended September 30, 2025. The rise was due to higher compensation expenses from increased headcount, professional services, legal expenses, and marketing expenses related to 'Toxic Avenger'.

Depreciation and Amortization: Amortization expenses increased due to higher capitalized content costs, reflecting the company's investment in content development.

Future Outlook: Cineverse Corp. plans to continue investing in content development and acquisitions, expecting to obtain appropriate returns on these investments. Management is focused on increasing revenue from existing services and offering new services to improve income from operations.

SEC Filing: Cineverse Corp. [ CNVS ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025
